title: Rollback cursor + dirty-timeline insert + partial/full replay
source: OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_timeline_rollback_dirty.cpp:92 — after adding 2 extrudes: operations().size()==2 AND appliedOpCount()==2.
OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_timeline_rollback_dirty.cpp:97-105 — RollbackCommand(op1) moves the applied cursor: appliedOpCount()==1 (rollback = cursor move, ops NOT deleted).

OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_timeline_rollback_dirty.cpp:107-129 — insert op3 at the rollback cursor: operations().size()==3; ORDER op1@index0, op3@index1, op2@index2; appliedOpCount()==2 (cursor covers only the inserted prefix).
OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_timeline_rollback_dirty.cpp:131-136 — regenerateToAppliedCount(2): status != CriticalFailure (partial regen of the applied prefix succeeds).

OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_timeline_rollback_dirty.cpp:138-143 — setAppliedOpCount(3) then regenerateToAppliedCount(3): status != CriticalFailure AND failedOps empty (full regen-to-end recovers).
expected-values/proto_timeline_rollback_dirty.txt — 'Timeline rollback/dirty prototype passed', 'regenerateAll:done status= 0 succeeded= 3 failed= 0 skipped= 0', exit 0.

opScript.note: Move applied cursor back to just-after op1 (op2 becomes a draft beyond the cursor).
Inserted AT the rollback cursor (between op1 and op2).

notes: Establishes cursor-based rollback semantics: rollback moves appliedOpCount WITHOUT deleting ops (drafts live beyond the cursor and preserve dependency links); inserting at the cursor renumbers order (op1,op3,op2) and keeps the applied prefix at 2; partial regen replays only the applied prefix; setting the cursor to end + full regen recovers all 3. In the NEW stack this maps to the linear timeline + StepState{Valid,Dirty,Error,NeedsRepair,Suppressed} with cursor rollback (plan: 'rollback = cursor, NOT C++ suppression conflation'). RegenStatus enum value 0 == Success (per stdout 'status= 0').
